skill.name: pr-description-writer
skill.description: Generates structured pull request descriptions with summaries, diagrams, file groupings, test plans, and reviewer-focused notes.
skill.summary: Creates reviewer-friendly PR descriptions from branch, diff, and GitHub PR context.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-cryptography patterns, but review shows the file is a Markdown skill with Git and GitHub CLI examples. The external command use is legitimate for PR description generation, but it can read local diffs and update or create GitHub PRs, so publication should include a permission warning. No evidence found of prompt injection, obfuscated payloads, malicious network endpoints, or credential exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Repository and PR Context Collection
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run git and gh commands that inspect branch history, diffs, status, files, commits, and PR metadata. This is necessary for the stated purpose, but it may expose sensitive uncommitted changes to the AI session. Verdict: true positive for external command and repository data access. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: the commands are explicit and semantically aligned with PR writing, but no hidden exfiltration endpoint was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and semantically aligned with PR writing, but no hidden exfiltration endpoint was found.

medium_findings.title: GitHub PR Mutation Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents gh pr edit and gh pr create commands that can modify or create pull requests on GitHub. This is expected behavior for a PR writer, but it should require user confirmation before execution. Verdict: true positive for external command use with remote side effects. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: the commands directly mutate GitHub PR state, while the surrounding text frames them as user-requested workflow steps.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands directly mutate GitHub PR state, while the surrounding text frames them as user-requested workflow steps.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography findings occur in prose, headings, Markdown examples, or ordinary PR-writing terms, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive algorithm implementation was found in the reviewed skill file. Verdict: false positive. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: the file is Markdown instructions and the cited context contains documentation text, not executable cryptographic logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown instructions and the cited context contains documentation text, not executable cryptographic logic.

low_findings.title: Device File and Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The filesystem matches are standard stderr redirects to /dev/null and branch-discovery commands used to select a PR base branch. The system-reconnaissance matches are Mermaid syntax and GitHub CLI examples, not host enumeration. Verdict: false positive with minor operational visibility. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: the cited lines show conventional Git workflow commands and documentation examples without privilege escalation or broad filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show conventional Git workflow commands and documentation examples without privilege escalation or broad filesystem traversal.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in PR Body Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples use shell command substitution with a quoted heredoc to pass generated Markdown to gh pr edit or gh pr create. The quoted heredoc reduces expansion risk, but the pattern still executes a remote state-changing command. Verdict: legitimate but sensitive command pattern. Confidence: 0.79. Confidence reasoning: the pattern is visible in the cited examples and has real side effects, but it is not malicious by itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible in the cited examples and has real side effects, but it is not malicious by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Comprehensive Diff Inspection Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow asks the agent to inspect full diffs, uncommitted changes, staged changes, and commit messages. This can expose sensitive source content in the AI context if the user runs it in a private repository. Verdict: expected capability with privacy implications. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: the commands are explicit and comprehensive, while no network exfiltration beyond normal GitHub CLI use was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and comprehensive, while no network exfiltration beyond normal GitHub CLI use was found.

content.value_statement: Large pull requests are hard to review when context is scattered across commits and diffs. This skill gathers Git and GitHub context, then drafts a clear PR description with summaries, diagrams, files changed, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the current branch already has a GitHub pull request.
Identifies a target base branch from PR metadata, repository defaults, or remote branch data.
Collects commit history, file changes, staged changes, unstaged changes, and PR metadata.
Drafts structured Markdown with summaries, feature tables, diagrams, files changed, tests, and notes.
Provides GitHub CLI commands for creating or updating a PR description.
limitations: Requires Git and GitHub CLI access for the full workflow.
Can only summarize changes visible in the current checkout and available PR metadata.
Generated descriptions still need human review for product intent and rollout details.
Commands that create or edit PRs should be run only after user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Create a reviewer-ready description for a branch with several related feature changes.

use_cases.title: Refresh an Existing PR
use_cases.description: Update a stale PR body after new commits, file changes, or testing details are added.

use_cases.title: Standardize Review Notes
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent summary, testing, files changed, and breaking changes structure across team PRs.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Current Branch PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete PR body.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-description-writer to create a PR description for my current branch. Include summary, files changed, testing, and notes.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing PR Description
prompt_templates.scenario: Refresh a PR after more commits were added.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-description-writer to review the existing PR and update the description so it covers all commits and current diffs.

prompt_templates.title: Focus Reviewers on Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Call out complex or risky changes for reviewers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-description-writer to draft a PR description that highlights risky areas, migration notes, manual tests, and reviewer focus points.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Structured Release Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a polished, release-aware PR body.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-description-writer to analyze the full branch against the base branch and produce a detailed PR description with diagrams, rollout notes, and rollback guidance.

output_examples.input: A branch adds a new API endpoint, updates frontend form validation, and includes related tests.
output: Summary: Adds the new endpoint and connects it to validated frontend input.
Key changes: API route, validation flow, shared types, and test coverage grouped by purpose.
Testing: Lists backend tests, frontend checks, and one manual request flow for reviewers.

output_examples.input: An existing PR has new commits that add a migration and change deployment requirements.
output: Summary: Updates the PR body to include the migration and deployment impact.
Notes: Adds explicit migration, rollback, and environment setup sections.
Reviewer focus: Calls out schema changes and files that need careful review.

best_practices: Review the generated PR body before running any GitHub CLI update command.
Include both automated and manual testing details when behavior changes are user-visible.
Call out migrations, feature flags, deployment steps, and breaking changes in dedicated sections.
anti_patterns: Do not publish a PR description that only summarizes the latest commit.
Do not hide breaking changes inside long prose or unrelated file lists.
Do not run PR creation or edit commands before confirming the target repository and base branch.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides GitHub CLI commands for creating or editing PRs, but users should confirm before running them.

faq.question: What tools does the workflow expect?
faq.answer: The full workflow expects Git, the GitHub CLI, and access to the repository branch being documented.

faq.question: Can it update an existing PR body?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect existing PR metadata and draft an updated body that preserves useful context.

faq.question: Does it include uncommitted changes?
faq.answer: The workflow checks staged and unstaged changes so the draft can reflect work that will be included.

faq.question: Can it produce diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends ASCII, Mermaid, or box diagrams when a flow is easier to review visually.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use care. The workflow reads diffs and PR metadata, so sensitive source changes may enter the AI context.
